cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out the answer to this question. The question is about a passage regarding the copyright of "Happy Birthday to You" and asks what the history experts' statements prove. The options are (A) to (D). Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand it. The main points are: - Jennifer Nelson had to pay $1,500 to use "Happy Birthday to You" in her documentary, which is about the history of the song. The money went to Warner Music Group, who claim they own the copyright since 1988. - The original song was written by the Hill sisters as "Good Morning to All" in 1893. People then started using the "Happy Birthday" lyrics with the same tune. - History experts say there's no record of who wrote the "Happy Birthday" lyrics and that the public was singing it long before it was printed
